summary: Partially decompiled and typed several functions in `src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.c`, with one full new match reported by decomp.dev: `lb_8001285C` at 100% (+312 bytes). The PR also improved `lb_80013E3C`, `lb_80014638`, and `lb_800115F4`, and reshaped related fighter/item fields into shared argument structs so callers match the newly understood `lb_80014638` interface.
what_changed: Implemented `lb_8001285C(HSD_ImageDesc*, GXTexObj*)`, setting up a texture object and GX state with calls such as `GXInitTexObj`, `GXClearVtxDesc`, `GXSetCullMode`, `GXSetNumTexGens`, `GXSetNumTevStages`, `GXLoadTexObj`, `GXSetVtxAttrFmt`, `GXSetTevOrder`, `GXSetTevColorIn`, `GXSetTevColorOp`, and `GXSetBlendMode`.
Added partial C implementations for `lb_800115F4`, `lb_80013E3C`, and `lb_80014638`, replacing previous unmatched stubs.

Added inline helpers in `lb_00F9.c`: `inlineB0` for linked-list/count update behavior in `lb_800115F4`, `inlineD0` for `ColorOverlay` light blend setup, and `inlineC0` for interpolation logic used by `lb_80014638`.
Changed `lb_800122C8` from unnamed/int parameters to `lb_800122C8(HSD_ImageDesc* image_desc, u16 origx, u16 origy, bool clear)` and forwarded to `HSD_ImageDescCopyFromEFB(..., clear, true)`.
Introduced `struct lb_80014638_arg0_t` containing two `Vec3` fields and `struct lb_80014638_arg1_t` containing two floats plus a `Vec3`, with static size assertions.
Replaced fighter damage fields `Vec3 x1930` and `Vec3 x193c` with `struct lb_80014638_arg0_t x1930`, updating users to access `.x0` and `.xC` subfields.
Replaced item fields `Vec2 xB54`, `xB5C`, `xB60`, and `xB64` with `struct lb_80014638_arg1_t xB54`, updating item collision/position code to use `unk_x`, `unk_y`, and `x8` subfields.
Changed `it_80274D04` to take `struct lb_80014638_arg1_t*` instead of `Vec2*`, matching the new shared argument struct.
Moved several lb-related structs from `lb_00F9.static.h` into `src/melee/lb/types.h`, leaving a newly inferred stack struct for `lb_80014638` in the static header.
Extended `ColorOverlay`'s `x8_ptr1` union by naming it `union ColorOverlay_x8_t` and adding a raw `u32 val` view used by `lb_80013E3C`.
Renamed `lb_80011A50_t` fields from raw `x28`/`x30` to `unk_count0`/`unk_count1`, and changed `x0` from `s8` to `u8`.
Adjusted `HSD_ImageDescCopyFromEFB`'s `sync` parameter type from `int` to `bool` in `tobj.c` and `tobj.h`.

Cleaned `extern/dolphin/include/dolphin/gx.h` includes by adding `// IWYU pragma: export`, sorting the GX exports, removing a duplicate `GXTev` include, and normalizing the `GXSetDrawSyncCallback` prototype formatting.
smart_moves: Modeled the data passed into `lb_80014638` as reusable structs instead of forcing it to look like unrelated `Vec3` or `Vec2` data. This made the fighter and item layouts line up with the function's actual memory access pattern.
Updated all known producers and consumers of the new `lb_80014638_arg0_t` and `lb_80014638_arg1_t` shapes in the same PR, preventing header/API drift between lb, fighter, and item code.
Used `STATIC_ASSERT` on newly introduced struct sizes, which is especially important because these structs replace adjacent raw fields in `Fighter` and `Item` layouts.
Kept partially understood names honest with `unk_x`, `unk_y`, `unk_count0`, and `unk_count1` rather than overclaiming semantics.
Added `PAD_STACK(4)` where needed in matched/near-matched functions, indicating stack-layout sensitivity was handled explicitly.
Preserved evidence of recurring inline/list behavior with `@todo inline appears in #lb_800115F4`, making future deduplication easier.
Changed wrapper and SDK-like prototypes to stronger `bool` types where the call sites use boolean constants, improving type clarity without changing behavior.
Marked umbrella GX includes with IWYU export pragmas so include-cleanup tooling does not incorrectly remove transitive SDK exports.
decomp_lessons: When a function appears to take a `Vec3*` or `Vec2*` but accesses a larger compound region, create a dedicated argument struct and then propagate that type back into owning game structs.

Replacing adjacent scalar fields with a single struct can unlock function signatures and matching in unrelated modules; here, item fields at `xB54` and fighter damage fields at `x1930` became the argument layouts for `lb_80014638`.
Partial matches can still justify broad type cleanup when the new types are backed by offsets, field usage, and static size checks.
For linked-list update/deallocation loops, modeling the free-list reinsertion inline can improve match quality while preserving the original control flow.
For color/packed command streams, a named union with both structured and raw integer views can match bitmask operations without losing typed access to color fields.
GX setup functions often require exact enum choices and call ordering; matching `lb_8001285C` depended on reconstructing the full render-state sequence, not just the texture-object initialization.
Header-only cleanup such as IWYU pragmas can be part of matching work when new implementations require broader SDK symbols through umbrella headers.
naming_conventions: `lb_80014638_arg0_t` and `lb_80014638_arg1_t` follow the project pattern of naming unknown argument structs after the consuming function address.
Uncertain fields remained explicitly unknown, e.g. `unk_x`, `unk_y`, `unk_count0`, and `unk_count1`, instead of being given speculative gameplay names.
Subfields preserving offsets use offset-style names such as `x0`, `x8`, and `xC` inside newly discovered structs.
Function prototypes in headers retained address comments such as `/* 014638 */` and refined parameter names only when the usage was clear, e.g. `image_desc`, `origx`, `origy`, and `clear`.
The named union `ColorOverlay_x8_t` matches the owning field `ColorOverlay::x8_ptr1` and supports both `light_color`, `light_rot`, and raw `val` access.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(4)` in `lb_8001285C` and `lb_80014638` to satisfy stack layout/codegen requirements.
Factored repeated or compiler-sensitive code into static inline helpers (`inlineB0`, `inlineC0`, `inlineD0`) rather than flattening everything into the parent functions.
Matched `lb_80014638` with local `Vec3` temporaries copied from argument structs, then applied small-threshold Z interpolation using `ABS(b->z - a->z) < 0.01f`.
Represented a raw packed color/count value as `arg0->x8_ptr1->val & 0x03FFFFFF` to support the arithmetic in `lb_80013E3C`.
Updated call signatures and owning structs together so pointer arithmetic/field offsets generated by callers agree with the callee's expected layout.

For `lb_800115F4`, reproduced the active-list traversal, decrementing `unk_count0`, incrementing `unk_count1`, removing expired nodes, and pushing them onto a free list before updating `lb_804D63B4` state.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 38.60% (+0.01%, +312 bytes).
The bot reported one new full match: `main/melee/lb/lb_00F9::lb_8001285C`, +312 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%.
The bot reported three improved unmatched functions: `lb_80013E3C` to 94.94% (+300 bytes), `lb_80014638` to 89.74% (+280 bytes), and `lb_800115F4` to 85.92% (+244 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `lb_80013E3C`, `lb_80014638`, or `lb_800115F4`.
Search for future renames of `lb_80014638_arg0_t` and `lb_80014638_arg1_t` once their gameplay meaning is known.
Check whether `unk_count0` and `unk_count1` in `lb_80011A50_t` receive semantic names in later lb/rumble/color-overlay work.
Look for later cleanup of the `@todo inline appears in #lb_800115F4` duplication.
Search for additional callers of `HSD_ImageDescCopyFromEFB` affected by the `sync` parameter becoming `bool`.
